sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, xml_content XML, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO test_data (id, xml_content, name, created_at) VALUES (1, '<root><child>Muuli</child><child>aasi</child></root>', 'Muuli', NOW()), (2, '<root><child>Giraffe</child><child>Elephant</child></root>', 'Giraffe', NOW());
SELECT concat((SELECT name FROM test_data WHERE id = 1), (SELECT name FROM test_data WHERE id = 2)) AS combined_name;
SELECT id, xpath_contains(xml_content, 'Muuli') AS contains_muuli FROM test_data WHERE xpath_contains(xml_content, 'Muuli') = true;
SELECT id, concat(name, ' ', (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE id > 1)) AS name_with_count FROM test_data GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, CONCAT(NULLIF(name, ''), ' is an animal') AS description FROM test_data WHERE xpath_contains(xml_content, 'child');
SELECT id, concat((SELECT xpath_contains(xml_content, 'Muuli')), ' - Is contained?') AS existence_check FROM test_data;
CREATE TABLE test_data_2 (ref_id INT, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_data(id));
SELECT td.id, concat(td.name, ' ', CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM test_data_2 WHERE ref_id = td.id) THEN 'exists' ELSE 'does not exist' END) AS existence_status FROM test_data td;
SELECT id, xpath_contains(concat('<root>', name, '</root>'), 'Muuli') AS xml_check FROM test_data;
